title: Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-901)
description: Sample question bank for the AI-901 certification. Used to develop and exercise the Tutor frontend before the authoring pipeline produces real packages.

sources.title: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-901) Master Cheat Sheet

domains.title: Implement AI solutions using Microsoft Foundry

concepts.title: Fairness in responsible AI
concepts.summary: An AI system should treat all people equitably, without bias.

grounding.text: Fairness means that an AI system treats all people equitably and does not introduce or amplify bias against any group.

concepts.title: Microsoft's responsible AI principles
concepts.summary: The six guiding principles: fairness, reliability & safety, privacy & security, inclusiveness, transparency, and accountability.

grounding.text: Microsoft defines six responsible AI principles: fairness, reliability and safety, privacy and security, inclusiveness, transparency, and accountability.

concepts.title: Types of machine learning
concepts.summary: Supervised (labelled data), unsupervised (unlabelled data), and reinforcement learning (reward-driven).

grounding.text: Supervised learning trains on labelled data; unsupervised learning finds structure in unlabelled data; reinforcement learning learns from rewards and penalties.

concepts.title: Azure AI services categories
concepts.summary: Prebuilt Azure AI services span vision, speech, language, and decision capabilities.

grounding.text: Azure AI services are grouped into vision, speech, language, and decision categories, each exposing prebuilt models via REST and SDKs.

questions.stem: An AI loan-approval model denies loans more often to one demographic group. Which responsible-AI principle is violated?

options.rationale: That concerns consistent, safe behaviour, not equitable treatment across groups.
Disparate outcomes across demographic groups is the defining fairness violation.
Transparency is about explainability and disclosure, not equitable outcomes.
That concerns protecting data, not outcome equity.

questions.explanation: Fairness requires equitable treatment across groups; biased denial rates are the textbook fairness violation.
hints: Which principle is specifically about equitable outcomes across people?

questions.stem: Which of the following are among Microsoft's six responsible AI principles? (Select all that apply.)

options.rationale: Inclusiveness is one of the six principles — AI should empower everyone.
Accountability is one of the six — people are answerable for AI systems.
Profitability is a business concern, not a responsible-AI principle.
Transparency is one of the six — systems should be understandable.
Scalability is an engineering property, not a responsible-AI principle.

questions.explanation: The six principles are fairness, reliability & safety, privacy & security, inclusiveness, transparency, and accountability. Profitability and scalability are not among them.
hints: There are exactly six principles — three of these five belong.

questions.stem: Unsupervised learning trains a model using data that has been labelled with the correct answers.

options.rationale: That describes supervised learning. Unsupervised learning uses unlabelled data.
Correct — unsupervised learning works on unlabelled data and discovers structure on its own.

questions.explanation: Labelled training data is the hallmark of supervised learning; unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabelled data.
hints: Which kind of learning needs the answers provided up front?

questions.stem: You need a prebuilt API that converts spoken audio into text. Which Azure AI services category should you use?

options.rationale: Vision handles images and video, not audio.
The Speech category provides speech-to-text (and text-to-speech).
Language analyses text that already exists; it does not transcribe audio.
Decision services (e.g. Anomaly Detector, Content Moderator) do not transcribe audio.

questions.explanation: Speech-to-text lives in the Speech category of Azure AI services. (Rendered as a dropdown to exercise that control type.)
hints: Match the modality of the input — audio — to the service category.
